key_points: Washburn is cheapest dorm; best for meeting people
Dorm quality is visibly low; students want to move out
Most students commute; campus housing is not the norm
key_quotes: I highly recommend living in Washburn. It's the cheapest dorm, and you can tell, but you're most likely to meet new people
'this is why i want to move' / 'thats why its so dirty and everyone wants to move out lmfao'
SJSU is a predominantly commuter school

key_points: Widely described as a commuter school with dead social scene
Introverts actually prefer it; low pressure, big-school feel
Awkward social dynamics noted; some students just hang around
key_quotes: every video i've watched is saying that the social life is kinda dead but i'm a major introvert so it's perfect
there are some weirdos here at sjsu. Mad awkward dudes that just hang around doing nothing, I swear no hobbies or anything
The COVID lockdown really messed up an entire generation's social skills

key_points: On-campus food is overpriced; students mock the pricing openly
Tables left filthy constantly; roach complaints linked to mess
Off-campus spots like Peanuts Cafe and La Victoria are beloved
key_quotes: Maybe y'all should take pride in your fucking pricing!!!
Then people wonder why there are roaches everywhere — its because people nasty
Peanuts is an SJSU institution. I used to get breakfast there all the time after my 9am classes

key_points: Burnout is widespread; students openly ask where to cry on campus
Wellness center appointments unavailable when students need them most
Community is surprisingly supportive on Reddit; peers help each other
key_quotes: This is my first semester at SJSU and I'm so emotionally and mentally drained, I can't find the will to go to class
No available appointments at the wellness center to confirm [strep throat]
it seems like too many of us are down in the dumps, do we need a hug meet up?

key_points: Tuition is ~$4,400/semester; one of cheapest options in California
Students accepted to Berkeley/UCLA still choose SJSU to avoid debt
IRA fee allocation heavily skewed toward athletics, students are frustrated
key_quotes: I got into cal for data science and made it in... i got fucked over for being lower middle class... i just feel like i worked four years of my ass off in high school for nothing. on the bright side, i got into sj's cs major and i'll be free of debt
Graduated with only $30k in total expenses as a commuter while any other college would've been $150k+
I paid off my student loan within 6 months

key_points: CS is no longer a safety — impacted, competitive, cutoffs rising yearly
Professor quality is inconsistent; half of lectures reportedly incomprehensible
AI detection false positives happening; professors refusing to hear appeals
key_quotes: I cannot understand the lectures at all for about half my classes. My peers report the same at least in my area of study
one of my professor's detected AI in one of my essays, even though i used 0 AI... she wouldn't even let me [show proof]
A great school for almost any major except computer science... many people I know in CS mention the lack of additional classes and the need for better professors

key_points: President's emails flagged as 100% AI-generated; students are furious
University aggressively pushing AI integration despite faculty and student resistance
Class cancellations happen a week before semester with no good alternatives
key_quotes: I put it into multiple AI checkers and they all returned 100% AI... Couldn't she have just written it herself?
And the CSU Board of Trustees just agreed to give her a pay raise with tuition dollars
i meticulously planned out my course schedule DURING MARCH/APRIL and I need this class as a prerequisite... all of the sudden it gets cancelled a week before the first day

key_points: Open campus means random non-students, scammers, and homeless enter freely
Parking is a nightmare and has been since at least 1998
Campus itself is compact and walkable with hidden art and library Easter eggs
key_quotes: one of the reasons i failed some classes in my freshman year at sjsu in 1994-95 was because of parking issues
It's always been like that [homeless in MLK library]. They come in for the bathrooms and free wifi
Keep your earbuds in and eyes laser-focused on the building you are briskly marching towards. Don't pause for anyone

key_points: Median starting salary for CS grads is $111k; location drives this
Silicon Valley proximity means direct tech recruiting from campus
Alumni network credited for landing Finance Director at Google, pilot roles, etc.
key_quotes: I graduated with a business degree in 2012 and I'm now a Finance Director at Google, all because I was able to leverage the alumni network
The median starting salary for SJS CS is 111k. Yes you read that correctly. 111k for fresh 22 year old cs grads
I really loved that college, I went there. Now I am a pilot for Hawaiian airlines thanks to them and their engineering department

key_points: Best ROI in California for CS/engineering given location and low cost
Tuition money wasted on $4,000 trash cans and AI Instagram posts
Students paying $4,500/semester still come to class sick to justify cost
key_quotes: I went to SJSU. There is virtually no research, but the students end up working in great companies, and its affordable
i've been seeing way more trash piling up since they added these I love my tuition getting raised to make campus worse
Or just wear a mask. Idgaf if I'm dying I paid $4500 for these classes and I'll be at every one, dead or alive

key_points: Pragmatic, career-focused, commuter school with minimal traditional college experience
Politically active campus with protests, firings, and genuine student advocacy
Students genuinely proud of scrappy underdog identity and outcomes
key_quotes: It just gets overshadowed by UC Berkeley, Stanford, Santa Clara... but for those who want to keep it simple and do what's needed... this is the best option
I remember watching this video 2 years back while I was starting my application... Currently I am studying at SJSU definitely it was worth all the hassle
Much of the public now has very negative associations with 'Silicon Valley'... I don't think the motto will age any better going forward

key_points: Safety: shooting threats, on-campus graffiti threats, multiple nearby murders in one week
Campus Security: open campus allows stalkers, scammers, and suspected traffickers near students
Administration: president uses AI-written emails, penalizes students for AI while doing it herself
key_quotes: 2 people killed basically on campus, like 10 yards from Philz. 5 people shot in San Pedro Square. That Hindu STUDENT severely assaulted by south garage. All in the last 48 hours
A woman wearing all black and black nitrile gloves... asked me to smell something... I was approached on the first day of classes
Someone give her a 0 — [And the CSU Board of Trustees just agreed to give her a pay raise with tuition dollars]

key_points: MLK Library has hidden art, revolving bookcase, tiny door, and owl — worth exploring
Peanuts Cafe off-campus is a beloved cheap institution for decades
BayPass/GoPass transit campaigns could eliminate $2,000/semester commute costs
key_quotes: That library has a ton of hidden art and stuff, there's articles written on it
man that place [Peanuts] was my home during my bachelors. cheap food, beer, good vibes
BayPass is 2x cheaper than GoPass at $50/yr ($25/sem) and covers BART, Caltrain, buses and ferries
